title: Leviticus: Offerings and Worship
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of leviticus: offerings and worship and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study leviticus: offerings and worship and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What is the Hebrew term for the offering in Leviticus in which the offerer, priest, and God all share in the meal? Answer: the peace offering, also called the fellowship offering or shelamim.
segments.correct_answer: peace offering — also called fellowship offering or shelamim
acceptable_variants: the peace offering where all parties eat
shelem — the communion sacrifice shared between offerer and priests

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The offering entirely consumed by fire on the altar, representing total consecration to God; the most common offering in Leviticus with no portion kept by the offerer
The offering required for unintentional sins and violations of sacred property; it included restitution plus twenty percent and a sacrifice
